name: liza-mas/liza
summary: Multi-agent coding system with Go supervisors, a behavioral contract, doer/reviewer roles, YAML blackboard state, worktree-managed tasks, and a code-enforced workflow state machine.
evidence: README frames Liza around hardened multi-agent coding, durable maintainability, and a behavioral contract that makes stuck states, approval requests, and evidence explicit.
The system pairs doer and reviewer roles across planning and coding stages, using submission, comments, verdicts, and revised submissions until approval.
Go supervisors manage isolated git worktrees, deterministic operations, task state transitions, blackboard YAML, handoff, recovery, and review flow validation.
Codex is supported through setup/init flags, embedded Codex configuration, provider-aware MCP/tool naming fixes, non-interactive execution fixes, and Codex demo traces.
